summary: Stunning Miller soundboard remaster captures a near‑perfect 1973 show highlighted by an epic Weather Report Suite → Dark Star → Eyes of the World sequence and crystal‑clear mix.
review: The 12‑18‑73 performance is hailed as one of the band’s finest, with the second‑set suite (Weather Report Suite, Dark Star, Drums, Eyes of the World, Wharf Rat) described as “jazzy,” “dialed in” and a benchmark for the year. The Miller remaster delivers brilliant clarity—crisp highs, deep lows and an almost studio‑like balance—though a brief AUD patch and some vocal distance are noted. Overall both the music and the transfer receive enthusiastic praise from the Deadhead community.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews, almost all 4‑5 stars, with consistent praise for both the musical performance and the high‑fidelity soundboard transfer; only minor technical quibbles noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered by Charlie Miller – described as crystal clear, crisp, brilliant and with superb low‑end. A one‑minute AUD patch is mentioned but deemed negligible; a few reviewers note the Wall of Sound vocal distance.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers repeatedly highlight seamless transitions, especially the WRS → Dark Star → Drums → Eyes → Wharf Rat sequence and the tight flow between China/​Rider and later jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring slide work, commanding second‑set solos, “best playing of the year,” and his energetic vocal presence.
band_member_comments.Phil: Repeatedly lauded for jazzy bass lines, “Phases of Phil,” and his melodic interplay during Weather Report Suite and Eyes of the World.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for strong lead guitar on Eyes of the World and solid rhythm/vocal contributions; no major criticism.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s organ/piano work described as front‑and‑center, jazzy, and a key driver of the WRS and Dark Star jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming praised for tightness and creative fills, especially during the drum solo that bridges into Eyes.
